definition: Administered energy pricing in EM creates a sawtooth inflation pattern with fiscal-FX nexus: energy prices surge → subsidy costs escalate → fiscal deficit widens → sovereign credit deterioration → FX pressure → subsidy becomes unsustainable → discrete overnight price shock. Timing is inherently unpredictable as it is politically determined.
evidence: Nigeria June 2023: fuel subsidy removal, petrol prices tripled overnight, naira -40%
Indonesia September 2022: pertalite adjustment +1.5pp CPI in one month
Egypt: phased subsidy removal producing recurring 3-5pp CPI spikes
No smooth-passthrough model captures this distinct transmission mechanism
open_questions: Can timing be predicted based on fiscal sustainability indicators?
Does IMF conditionality accelerate the subsidy-removal cycle?
How do international oil price hedging programs (e.g., Mexico) alter the dynamics?
